title: Garlic Gets Its Glory Day!
story: Every April 19, garlic lovers everywhere celebrate National Garlic Day by cooking up a storm! People roast whole garlic bulbs until they're sweet and creamy, make garlic bread that fills their kitchens with amazing smells, and even try garlic ice cream if they're feeling brave. Restaurants create special garlic-heavy menus, and food bloggers share their stinkiest recipes online. Some families have garlic cook-offs to see who can make the most delicious dish using this powerful little bulb that keeps vampires away!
activity_question: What do some brave people try eating on National Garlic Day?
bonus_fact: One garlic bulb can contain up to 20 individual cloves inside!

title: Rolling Into Rice Ball Day!
story: Every April 19, people celebrate National Rice Ball Day by making and eating these tasty treats! Rice balls, called onigiri in Japan, are sticky rice shaped into triangles or balls and often filled with salmon, tuna, or pickled plums. Today, families roll up their sleeves to make homemade versions, while others buy fancy ones from Japanese restaurants. Some people get creative with fun fillings like cheese or chicken. It's the perfect day to try this simple but delicious snack!
activity_question: What are rice balls called in Japan?
follow_up_question: What creative filling would you put in your own rice ball?

title: Bicycle Day: Pedaling Into Fun!
story: Every April 19, cyclists around the world celebrate Bicycle Day by hopping on their bikes for special rides and events! This quirky holiday honors the amazing two-wheeled machines that have been getting people around for over a century. On this day, bike shops offer free tune-ups, cities organize group rides through parks and neighborhoods, and families dust off their helmets for fun adventures together. Some people decorate their bikes with colorful streamers, bells, and lights to make their rides extra festive. Schools might host bike safety workshops or organize races on playgrounds. Whether you're cruising on a mountain bike, racing on a road bike, or just enjoying a leisurely ride around the block, Bicycle Day reminds everyone how awesome it feels to pedal under your own power!
activity_question: What do some people add to their bikes to make Bicycle Day rides more festive?
bonus_fact: The first bicycle was invented in 1817 and didn't even have pedals!

title: Pretty Primrose Day Petals
story: People across Britain wear bright yellow primrose flowers on April 19 to celebrate Primrose Day! This cheerful holiday honors a famous British leader who loved these delicate spring blooms. Families pick fresh primroses from gardens and countryside paths, then pin them to their clothes or carry small bouquets. Some people leave primrose wreaths at statues and memorials throughout the day.
activity_question: What do people wear on Primrose Day?
follow_up_question: What other flowers could represent your favorite season?

title: Tulips and Friendship: Dutch-American Day
story: Every April 19, people celebrate Dutch-American Friendship Day to honor the special bond between the Netherlands and the United States. Dutch communities across America host festivals with traditional wooden shoe dancing, tulip displays, and delicious stroopwafels. Many families share stories about their Dutch heritage while enjoying pancakes and hot chocolate. It's a perfect day to learn about windmills, visit Dutch museums, or simply appreciate how two countries can stay friends across the ocean!
activity_question: What traditional Dutch treat do people often enjoy on Dutch-American Friendship Day?
bonus_fact: The Netherlands gave the Statue of Liberty's crown design inspiration to America!
